reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: I know my own answer (73 - 29) is correct, and based on Game 1 all other players solved similarly simple arithmetic puzzles correctly. The chance that one of them messed up seems lower than the risk of another random elimination if everyone waits again. Verifying now would cost a coin and still leave open the possibility that nobody volunteers this round. Volunteering immediately both preserves my coins and prevents further random deaths, with a high likelihood that all four answers are correct.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: All four puzzles are simple arithmetic, and in the previous game every player’s initial answer was correct—verification only cost coins and caused a random death when no one volunteered. Waiting (by passing or verifying) keeps the random-elimination risk without meaningfully improving the already very high likelihood that A, C, and D solved their questions correctly. By volunteering now using each player’s own capsule (Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D), I avoid random elimination entirely and preserve my 4 coins, with extremely low risk that the password is wrong.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Verify: Q1  
REASONING: Every question currently has only a single, unverified answer, so volunteering now would be very risky. Verifying Q1 (A’s puzzle) spends 1 coin but increases group confidence in that part of the password, making a safe volunteer more likely in later rounds while still avoiding a pure “pass” that gives no new information.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.
